NKJV.1: “Then the kingdom of heaven shall be likened to ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet  the bridegroom.
NKJV.2: Now five of them were wise, and five were foolish.
NKJV.3: Those who were foolish took their lamps and took no oil with them,
NKJV.4: but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
NKJV.5: But while the bridegroom was delayed,  they all slumbered and slept.
NKJV.6: “And at midnight a cry was heard: ‘Behold, the bridegroom is coming; go out to meet him!’
NKJV.7: Then all those virgins arose and  trimmed their lamps.
NKJV.8: And the foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
NKJV.9: But the wise answered, saying, ‘No, lest there should not be enough for us and you; but go rather to those who sell, and buy for yourselves.’
NKJV.10: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding; and  the door was shut.
NKJV.11: “Afterward the other virgins came also, saying,  ‘Lord, Lord, open to us!’
NKJV.12: But he answered and said, ‘Assuredly, I say to you,  I do not know you.’
NKJV.13: “Watch therefore, for you  know neither the day nor the hour in which the Son of Man is coming.
NKJV.14: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a man traveling to a far country, who called his own servants and delivered his goods to them.
NKJV.15: And to one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one,  to each according to his own ability; and immediately he went on a journey.
NKJV.16: Then he who had received the five talents went and traded with them, and made another five talents.
NKJV.17: And likewise he who had received two gained two more also.
NKJV.18: But he who had received one went and dug in the ground, and hid his lord’s money.
NKJV.19: After a long time the lord of those servants came and settled accounts with them.
NKJV.20: “So he who had received five talents came and brought five other talents, saying, ‘Lord, you delivered to me five talents; look, I have gained five more talents besides them.’
NKJV.21: His lord said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant; you were faithful over a few things, I will make you ruler over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.’
NKJV.22: He also who had received two talents came and said, ‘Lord, you delivered to me two talents; look, I have gained two more talents besides them.’
NKJV.23: His lord said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant; you have been faithful over a few things, I will make you ruler over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.’
NKJV.24: “Then he who had received the one talent came and said, ‘Lord, I knew you to be a hard man, reaping where you have not sown, and gathering where you have not scattered seed.
NKJV.25: And I was afraid, and went and hid your talent in the ground. Look, there you have what is yours.’
NKJV.26: “But his lord answered and said to him, ‘You  wicked and lazy servant, you knew that I reap where I have not sown, and gather where I have not scattered seed.
NKJV.27: So you ought to have deposited my money with the bankers, and at my coming I would have received back my own with interest.
NKJV.28: Therefore take the talent from him, and give it to him who has ten talents.
NKJV.29: ‘For to everyone who has, more will be given, and he will have abundance; but from him who does not have, even what he has will be taken away.
NKJV.30: And cast the unprofitable servant  into the outer darkness.  There will be weeping and  gnashing of teeth.’
NKJV.31: “When the Son of Man comes in His glory, and all the holy angels with Him, then He will sit on the throne of His glory.
NKJV.32: All the nations will be gathered before Him, and He will separate them one from another, as a shepherd divides his sheep from the goats.
NKJV.33: And He will set the  sheep on His right hand, but the goats on the left.
NKJV.34: Then the King will say to those on His right hand, ‘Come, you blessed of My Father,  inherit the kingdom  prepared for you from the foundation of the world:
NKJV.35: for I was hungry and you gave Me food; I was thirsty and you gave Me drink;  I was a stranger and you took Me in;
NKJV.36: I was naked and you clothed Me; I was sick and you visited Me; I was in prison and you came to Me.’
NKJV.37: “Then the righteous will answer Him, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see You hungry and feed You, or thirsty and give You drink?
NKJV.38: When did we see You a stranger and take You in, or naked and clothe You?
NKJV.39: Or when did we see You sick, or in prison, and come to You?’
NKJV.40: And the King will answer and say to them, ‘Assuredly, I say to you, inasmuch as you did it to one of the least of these My brethren, you did it to Me.’
NKJV.41: “Then He will also say to those on the left hand,  ‘Depart from Me, you cursed,  into the everlasting fire prepared for  the devil and his angels:
NKJV.42: for I was hungry and you gave Me no food; I was thirsty and you gave Me no drink;
NKJV.43: I was a stranger and you did not take Me in, naked and you did not clothe Me, sick and in prison and you did not visit Me.’
NKJV.44: “Then they also will answer Him, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see You hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and did not minister to You?’
NKJV.45: Then He will answer them, saying, ‘Assuredly, I say to you, inasmuch as you did not do it to one of the least of these, you did not do it to Me.’
NKJV.46: And  these will go away into everlasting punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”

ESV.1: “Then the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps  and went to meet the bridegroom.
ESV.2: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
ESV.3: For when the foolish took their lamps, they took no oil with them,
ESV.4: but the wise took flasks of oil with their lamps.
ESV.5: As the bridegroom was delayed, they all became drowsy and slept.
ESV.6: But at midnight there was a cry, ‘Here is the bridegroom! Come out to meet him.’
ESV.7: Then all those virgins rose and trimmed their lamps.
ESV.8: And the foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
ESV.9: But the wise answered, saying, ‘Since there will not be enough for us and for you, go rather to the dealers and buy for yourselves.’
ESV.10: And while they were going to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the marriage feast, and the door was shut.
ESV.11: Afterward the other virgins came also, saying, ‘Lord, lord, open to us.’
ESV.12: But he answered, ‘Truly, I say to you, I do not know you.’
ESV.13: Watch therefore, for you know neither the day nor the hour.
ESV.14: “For it will be like a man going on a journey, who called his servants  and entrusted to them his property.
ESV.15: To one he gave five talents,  to another two, to another one, to each according to his ability. Then he went away.
ESV.16: He who had received the five talents went at once and traded with them, and he made five talents more.
ESV.17: So also he who had the two talents made two talents more.
ESV.18: But he who had received the one talent went and dug in the ground and hid his master's money.
ESV.19: Now after a long time the master of those servants came and settled accounts with them.
ESV.20: And he who had received the five talents came forward, bringing five talents more, saying, ‘Master, you delivered to me five talents; here, I have made five talents more.’
ESV.21: His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant.  You have been faithful over a little; I will set you over much. Enter into the joy of your master.’
ESV.22: And he also who had the two talents came forward, saying, ‘Master, you delivered to me two talents; here, I have made two talents more.’
ESV.23: His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a little; I will set you over much. Enter into the joy of your master.’
ESV.24: He also who had received the one talent came forward, saying, ‘Master, I knew you to be a hard man, reaping where you did not sow, and gathering where you scattered no seed,
ESV.25: so I was afraid, and I went and hid your talent in the ground. Here, you have what is yours.’
ESV.26: But his master answered him, ‘You wicked and slothful servant! You knew that I reap where I have not sown and gather where I scattered no seed?
ESV.27: Then you ought to have invested my money with the bankers, and at my coming I should have received what was my own with interest.
ESV.28: So take the talent from him and give it to him who has the ten talents.
ESV.29: For to everyone who has will more be given, and he will have an abundance. But from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken away.
ESV.30: And cast the worthless servant into the outer darkness. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
ESV.31: “When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, then he will sit on his glorious throne.
ESV.32: Before him will be gathered all the nations, and he will separate people one from another as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
ESV.33: And he will place the sheep on his right, but the goats on the left.
ESV.34: Then the King will say to those on his right, ‘Come, you who are blessed by my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
ESV.35: For I was hungry and you gave me food, I was thirsty and you gave me drink, I was a stranger and you welcomed me,
ESV.36: I was naked and you clothed me, I was sick and you visited me, I was in prison and you came to me.’
ESV.37: Then the righteous will answer him, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you drink?
ESV.38: And when did we see you a stranger and welcome you, or naked and clothe you?
ESV.39: And when did we see you sick or in prison and visit you?’
ESV.40: And the King will answer them, ‘Truly, I say to you, as you did it to one of the least of these my brothers,  you did it to me.’
ESV.41: “Then he will say to those on his left, ‘Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels.
ESV.42: For I was hungry and you gave me no food, I was thirsty and you gave me no drink,
ESV.43: I was a stranger and you did not welcome me, naked and you did not clothe me, sick and in prison and you did not visit me.’
ESV.44: Then they also will answer, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and did not minister to you?’
ESV.45: Then he will answer them, saying, ‘Truly, I say to you, as you did not do it to one of the least of these, you did not do it to me.’
ESV.46: And these will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”

NASB.1: 'Then the kingdom of heaven will be comparable to ten virgins, who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
NASB.2: 'Five of them were foolish, and five were prudent.
NASB.3: 'For when the foolish took their lamps, they took no oil with them,
NASB.4: but the prudent took oil in flasks along with their lamps.
NASB.5: 'Now while the bridegroom was delaying, they all got drowsy and [began] to sleep.
NASB.6: 'But at midnight there was a shout, 'Behold, the bridegroom! Come out to meet [him].'
NASB.7: 'Then all those virgins rose and trimmed their lamps.
NASB.8: 'The foolish said to the prudent, 'Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.'
NASB.9: 'But the prudent answered, 'No, there will not be enough for us and you [too]; go instead to the dealers and buy [some] for yourselves.'
NASB.10: 'And while they were going away to make the purchase, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding feast; and the door was shut.
NASB.11: 'Later the other virgins also came, saying, 'Lord, lord, open up for us.'
NASB.12: 'But he answered, 'Truly I say to you, I do not know you.'
NASB.13: 'Be on the alert then, for you do not know the day nor the hour.
NASB.14: 'For [it is] just like a man [about] to go on a journey, who called his own slaves and entrusted his possessions to them.
NASB.15: 'To one he gave five talents, to another, two, and to another, one, each according to his own ability; and he went on his journey.
NASB.16: 'Immediately the one who had received the five talents went and traded with them, and gained five more talents.
NASB.17: 'In the same manner the one who [had received] the two [talents] gained two more.
NASB.18: 'But he who received the one [talent] went away, and dug [a hole] in the ground and hid his master's money.
NASB.19: 'Now after a long time the master of those slaves came and settled accounts with them.
NASB.20: 'The one who had received the five talents came up and brought five more talents, saying, 'Master, you entrusted five talents to me. See, I have gained five more talents.'
NASB.21: 'His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful slave. You were faithful with a few things, I will put you in charge of many things; enter into the joy of your master.'
NASB.22: 'Also the one who [had received] the two talents came up and said, 'Master, you entrusted two talents to me. See, I have gained two more talents.'
NASB.23: 'His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful slave. You were faithful with a few things, I will put you in charge of many things; enter into the joy of your master.'
NASB.24: 'And the one also who had received the one talent came up and said, 'Master, I knew you to be a hard man, reaping where you did not sow and gathering where you scattered no [seed].
NASB.25: 'And I was afraid, and went away and hid your talent in the ground. See, you have what is yours.'
NASB.26: 'But his master answered and said to him, 'You wicked, lazy slave, you knew that I reap where I did not sow and gather where I scattered no [seed].
NASB.27: 'Then you ought to have put my money in the bank, and on my arrival I would have received my [money] back with interest.
NASB.28: 'Therefore take away the talent from him, and give it to the one who has the ten talents.'
NASB.29: 'For to everyone who has, [more] shall be given, and he will have an abundance; but from the one who does not have, even what he does have shall be taken away.
NASB.30: 'Throw out the worthless slave into the outer darkness; in that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
NASB.31: 'But when the Son of Man comes in His glory, and all the angels with Him, then He will sit on His glorious throne.
NASB.32: 'All the nations will be gathered before Him; and He will separate them from one another, as the shepherd separates the sheep from the goats;
NASB.33: and He will put the sheep on His right, and the goats on the left.
NASB.34: 'Then the King will say to those on His right, 'Come, you who are blessed of My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
NASB.35: 'For I was hungry, and you gave Me [something] to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave Me [something] to drink; I was a stranger, and you invited Me in;
NASB.36: naked, and you clothed Me; I was sick, and you visited Me; I was in prison, and you came to Me.'
NASB.37: 'Then the righteous will answer Him, 'Lord, when did we see You hungry, and feed You, or thirsty, and give You [something] to drink?
NASB.38: 'And when did we see You a stranger, and invite You in, or naked, and clothe You?
NASB.39: 'When did we see You sick, or in prison, and come to You?'
NASB.40: 'The King will answer and say to them, 'Truly I say to you, to the extent that you did it to one of these brothers of Mine, [even] the least [of them], you did it to Me.'
NASB.41: 'Then He will also say to those on His left, 'Depart from Me, accursed ones, into the eternal fire which has been prepared for the devil and his angels;
NASB.42: for I was hungry, and you gave Me [nothing] to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave Me nothing to drink;
NASB.43: I was a stranger, and you did not invite Me in; naked, and you did not clothe Me; sick, and in prison, and you did not visit Me.'
NASB.44: 'Then they themselves also will answer, 'Lord, when did we see You hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not take care of You?'
NASB.45: 'Then He will answer them, 'Truly I say to you, to the extent that you did not do it to one of the least of these, you did not do it to Me.'
NASB.46: 'These will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.'

NLT.1: “Then the Kingdom of Heaven will be like ten bridesmaids who took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom.
NLT.2: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
NLT.3: The five who were foolish didn’t take enough olive oil for their lamps,
NLT.4: but the other five were wise enough to take along extra oil.
NLT.5: When the bridegroom was delayed, they all became drowsy and fell asleep.
NLT.6: “At midnight they were roused by the shout, ‘Look, the bridegroom is coming! Come out and meet him!’
NLT.7: “All the bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
NLT.8: Then the five foolish ones asked the others, ‘Please give us some of your oil because our lamps are going out.’
NLT.9: “But the others replied, ‘We don’t have enough for all of us. Go to a shop and buy some for yourselves.’
NLT.10: “But while they were gone to buy oil, the bridegroom came. Then those who were ready went in with him to the marriage feast, and the door was locked.
NLT.11: Later, when the other five bridesmaids returned, they stood outside, calling, ‘Lord! Lord! Open the door for us!’
NLT.12: “But he called back, ‘Believe me, I don’t know you!’
NLT.13: “So you, too, must keep watch! For you do not know the day or hour of my return.
NLT.14: “Again, the Kingdom of Heaven can be illustrated by the story of a man going on a long trip. He called together his servants and entrusted his money to them while he was gone.
NLT.15: He gave five bags of silver to one, two bags of silver to another, and one bag of silver to the last — dividing it in proportion to their abilities. He then left on his trip.
NLT.16: “The servant who received the five bags of silver began to invest the money and earned five more.
NLT.17: The servant with two bags of silver also went to work and earned two more.
NLT.18: But the servant who received the one bag of silver dug a hole in the ground and hid the master’s money.
NLT.19: “After a long time their master returned from his trip and called them to give an account of how they had used his money.
NLT.20: The servant to whom he had entrusted the five bags of silver came forward with five more and said, ‘Master, you gave me five bags of silver to invest, and I have earned five more.’
NLT.21: “The master was full of praise. ‘Well done, my good and faithful servant. You have been faithful in handling this small amount, so now I will give you many more responsibilities. Let’s celebrate together! ’
NLT.22: “The servant who had received the two bags of silver came forward and said, ‘Master, you gave me two bags of silver to invest, and I have earned two more.’
NLT.23: “The master said, ‘Well done, my good and faithful servant. You have been faithful in handling this small amount, so now I will give you many more responsibilities. Let’s celebrate together!’
NLT.24: “Then the servant with the one bag of silver came and said, ‘Master, I knew you were a harsh man, harvesting crops you didn’t plant and gathering crops you didn’t cultivate.
NLT.25: I was afraid I would lose your money, so I hid it in the earth. Look, here is your money back.’
NLT.26: “But the master replied, ‘You wicked and lazy servant! If you knew I harvested crops I didn’t plant and gathered crops I didn’t cultivate,
NLT.27: why didn’t you deposit my money in the bank? At least I could have gotten some interest on it.’
NLT.28: “Then he ordered, ‘Take the money from this servant, and give it to the one with the ten bags of silver.
NLT.29: To those who use well what they are given, even more will be given, and they will have an abundance. But from those who do nothing, even what little they have will be taken away.
NLT.30: Now throw this useless servant into outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
NLT.31: “But when the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, then he will sit upon his glorious throne.
NLT.32: All the nations will be gathered in his presence, and he will separate the people as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
NLT.33: He will place the sheep at his right hand and the goats at his left.
NLT.34: “Then the King will say to those on his right, ‘Come, you who are blessed by my Father, inherit the Kingdom prepared for you from the creation of the world.
NLT.35: For I was hungry, and you fed me. I was thirsty, and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger, and you invited me into your home.
NLT.36: I was naked, and you gave me clothing. I was sick, and you cared for me. I was in prison, and you visited me.’
NLT.37: “Then these righteous ones will reply, ‘Lord, when did we ever see you hungry and feed you? Or thirsty and give you something to drink?
NLT.38: Or a stranger and show you hospitality? Or naked and give you clothing?
NLT.39: When did we ever see you sick or in prison and visit you?’
NLT.40: “And the King will say, ‘I tell you the truth, when you did it to one of the least of these my brothers and sisters, you were doing it to me!’
NLT.41: “Then the King will turn to those on the left and say, ‘Away with you, you cursed ones, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his demons.
NLT.42: For I was hungry, and you didn’t feed me. I was thirsty, and you didn’t give me a drink.
NLT.43: I was a stranger, and you didn’t invite me into your home. I was naked, and you didn’t give me clothing. I was sick and in prison, and you didn’t visit me.’
NLT.44: “Then they will reply, ‘Lord, when did we ever see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and not help you?’
NLT.45: “And he will answer, ‘I tell you the truth, when you refused to help the least of these my brothers and sisters, you were refusing to help me.’
NLT.46: “And they will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous will go into eternal life.”

WEB.1: “Then the Kingdom of Heaven will be like ten virgins, who took their lamps, and went out to meet the bridegroom.
WEB.2: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
WEB.3: Those who were foolish, when they took their lamps, took no oil with them,
WEB.4: but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
WEB.5: Now while the bridegroom delayed, they all slumbered and slept.
WEB.6: But at midnight there was a cry, ‘Behold! The bridegroom is coming! Come out to meet him!’
WEB.7: Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.
WEB.8: The foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
WEB.9: But the wise answered, saying, ‘What if there isn’t enough for us and you? You go rather to those who sell, and buy for yourselves.’
WEB.10: While they went away to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the marriage feast, and the door was shut.
WEB.11: Afterward the other virgins also came, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us.’
WEB.12: But he answered, ‘Most certainly I tell you, I don’t know you.’
WEB.13: Watch therefore, for you don’t know the day nor the hour in which the Son of Man is coming.
WEB.14: “For it is like a man, going into another country, who called his own servants, and entrusted his goods to them.
WEB.15: To one he gave five talents,  to another two, to another one; to each according to his own ability. Then he went on his journey.
WEB.16: Immediately he who received the five talents went and traded with them, and made another five talents.
WEB.17: In the same way, he also who got the two gained another two.
WEB.18: But he who received the one talent went away and dug in the earth, and hid his lord’s money.
WEB.19: “Now after a long time the lord of those servants came, and reconciled accounts with them.
WEB.20: He who received the five talents came and brought another five talents, saying, ‘Lord, you delivered to me five talents. Behold, I have gained another five talents besides them.’
WEB.21: “His lord said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a few things, I will set you over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.’
WEB.22: “He also who got the two talents came and said, ‘Lord, you delivered to me two talents. Behold, I have gained another two talents besides them.’
WEB.23: “His lord said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a few things, I will set you over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.’
WEB.24: “He also who had received the one talent came and said, ‘Lord, I knew you that you are a hard man, reaping where you did not sow, and gathering where you did not scatter.
WEB.25: I was afraid, and went away and hid your talent in the earth. Behold, you have what is yours.’
WEB.26: “But his lord answered him, ‘You wicked and slothful servant. You knew that I reap where I didn’t sow, and gather where I didn’t scatter.
WEB.27: You ought therefore to have deposited my money with the bankers, and at my coming I should have received back my own with interest.
WEB.28: Take away therefore the talent from him, and give it to him who has the ten talents.
WEB.29: For to everyone who has will be given, and he will have abundance, but from him who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away.
WEB.30: Throw out the unprofitable servant into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
WEB.31: “But when the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then he will sit on the throne of his glory.
WEB.32: Before him all the nations will be gathered, and he will separate them one from another, as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
WEB.33: He will set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.
WEB.34: Then the King will tell those on his right hand, ‘Come, blessed of my Father, inherit the Kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world;
WEB.35: for I was hungry, and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty, and you gave me drink. I was a stranger, and you took me in.
WEB.36: I was naked, and you clothed me. I was sick, and you visited me. I was in prison, and you came to me.’
WEB.37: “Then the righteous will answer him, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry, and feed you; or thirsty, and give you a drink?
WEB.38: When did we see you as a stranger, and take you in; or naked, and clothe you?
WEB.39: When did we see you sick, or in prison, and come to you?’
WEB.40: “The King will answer them, ‘Most certainly I tell you, because you did it to one of the least of these my brothers,  you did it to me.’
WEB.41: Then he will say also to those on the left hand, ‘Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire which is prepared for the devil and his angels;
WEB.42: for I was hungry, and you didn’t give me food to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave me no drink;
WEB.43: I was a stranger, and you didn’t take me in; naked, and you didn’t clothe me; sick, and in prison, and you didn’t visit me.’
WEB.44: “Then they will also answer, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and didn’t help you?’
WEB.45: “Then he will answer them, saying, ‘Most certainly I tell you, because you didn’t do it to one of the least of these, you didn’t do it to me.’
WEB.46: These will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”

CSB17.7: “Then all the virgins got up and trimmed their lamps.
CSB17.8: The foolish ones said to the wise ones, ‘Give us some of your oil, because our lamps are going out.’

CSB17.13: “Therefore be alert, because you don’t know either the day or the hour. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.14: “For it is just like a man about to go on a journey. <sup>ⓗ</sup> He called his own servants and entrusted his possessions to them.
CSB17.15: To one he gave five talents, <sup>[4]</sup> to another two talents, and to another one talent, depending on each one’s ability. Then he went on a journey. <sup>ⓘ</sup>Immediately
CSB17.16: the man who had received five talents went, put them to work, and earned five more.
CSB17.17: In the same way the man with two earned two more.
CSB17.18: But the man who had received one talent went off, dug a hole in the ground, and hid his master’s money.
CSB17.19: “After a long time the master of those servants came and settled accounts with them.
CSB17.20: The man who had received five talents approached, presented five more talents, and said, ‘Master, you gave me five talents. See, I’ve earned five more talents.’
CSB17.21: “His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant! You were faithful over a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Share your master’s joy.’ <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.22: “The man with two talents also approached. He said, ‘Master, you gave me two talents. See, I’ve earned two more talents.’
CSB17.23: “His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant! You were faithful over a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Share your master’s joy.’
CSB17.24: “The man who had received one talent also approached and said, ‘Master, I know you. You’re a harsh man, reaping where you haven’t sown and gathering where you haven’t scattered seed. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.25: So I was afraid and went off and hid your talent in the ground. See, you have what is yours.’
CSB17.26: “His master replied to him, ‘You evil, lazy servant! <sup>ⓛ</sup> If you knew that I reap where I haven’t sown and gather where I haven’t scattered,
CSB17.27: then <sup>[5]</sup> you should have deposited my money with the bankers, and I would have received my money <sup>[6]</sup> back with interest when I returned.
CSB17.28: “‘So take the talent from him and give it to the one who has ten talents.
CSB17.29: For to everyone who has, more will be given, and he will have more than enough. But from the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.30: And throw this good-for-nothing servant into the outer darkness, <sup>ⓝ</sup> where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
CSB17.31: “When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup> with him, then he will sit on his glorious throne. <sup>ⓑ</sup>

CSB17.39: When did we see you sick, or in prison, and visit you?’

AMP.1: "Then the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins, who took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom.
AMP.2: Five of them were foolish [thoughtless, silly, and careless], and five were wise [far-sighted, practical, and sensible].
AMP.3: For when the foolish took their lamps, they did not take any [extra] oil with them,
AMP.4: but the wise took flasks of oil along with their lamps.
AMP.5: Now while the bridegroom was delayed, they all began to nod off, and they fell asleep.
AMP.6: But at midnight there was a shout, 'Look! The bridegroom [is coming]! Go out to meet him.'
AMP.7: Then all those virgins got up and put their own lamps in order [trimmed the wicks and added oil and lit them].
AMP.8: But the foolish virgins said to the wise, 'Give us some of your oil, because our lamps are going out.'
AMP.9: But the wise replied, 'No, otherwise there will not be enough for us and for you, too; go instead to the dealers and buy oil for yourselves.'
AMP.10: But while they were going away to buy oil, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding feast; and the door was shut and locked.
AMP.11: Later the others also came, and said, 'Lord, Lord, open [the door] for us.'
AMP.12: But He replied, 'I assure you and most solemnly say to you, I do not know you [we have no relationship].'
AMP.13: Therefore, be on the alert [be prepared and ready], for you do not know the day nor the hour [when the Son of Man will come].
AMP.14: "For it is just like a man who was about to take a journey, and he called his servants together and entrusted them with his possessions.
AMP.15: To one he gave five talents, to another, two, and to another, one, each according to his own ability; and then he went on his journey.
AMP.16: The one who had received the five talents went at once and traded with them, and he [made a profit and] gained five more.
AMP.17: Likewise the one who had two [made a profit and] gained two more.
AMP.18: But the one who had received the one went and dug a hole in the ground and hid his master's money.
AMP.19: "Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
AMP.20: And the one who had received the five talents came and brought him five more, saying, 'Master, you entrusted to me five talents. See, I have [made a profit and] gained five more talents.'
AMP.21: His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful and trustworthy over a little, I will put you in charge of many things; share in the joy of your master.'
AMP.22: "Also the one who had the two talents came forward, saying, 'Master, you entrusted two talents to me. See, I have [made a profit and] gained two more talents.'
AMP.23: His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful and trustworthy over a little, I will put you in charge of many things; share in the joy of your master.'
AMP.24: "The one who had received one talent also came forward, saying, 'Master, I knew you to be a harsh and demanding man, reaping [the harvest] where you did not sow and gathering where you did not scatter seed.
AMP.25: So I was afraid [to lose the talent], and I went and hid your talent in the ground. See, you have what is your own.'
AMP.26: "But his master answered him, 'You wicked, lazy servant, you knew that I reap [the harvest] where I did not sow and gather where I did not scatter seed.
AMP.27: Then you ought to have put my money with the bankers, and at my return I would have received my money back with interest.
AMP.28: So take the talent away from him, and give it to the one who has the ten talents.'
AMP.29: "For to everyone who has [and values his blessings and gifts from God, and has used them wisely], more will be given, and [he will be richly supplied so that] he will have an abundance; but from the one who does not have [because he has ignored or disregarded his blessings and gifts from God], even what he does have will be taken away.
AMP.30: And throw out the worthless servant into the outer darkness; in that place [of grief and torment] there will be weeping [over sorrow and pain] and grinding of teeth [over distress and anger].
AMP.31: "But when the Son of Man comes in His glory and majesty and all the angels with Him, then He will sit on the throne of His glory.
AMP.32: All the nations will be gathered before Him [for judgment]; and He will separate them from one another, as a shepherd separates his sheep from the goats;
AMP.33: and He will put the sheep on His right [the place of honor], and the goats on His left [the place of rejection].
AMP.34: "Then the King will say to those on His right, 'Come, you blessed of My Father [you favored of God, appointed to eternal salvation], inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
AMP.35: For I was hungry, and you gave Me something to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave Me something to drink; I was a stranger, and you invited Me in;
AMP.36: I was naked, and you clothed Me; I was sick, and you visited Me [with help and ministering care]; I was in prison, and you came to Me [ignoring personal danger].'
AMP.37: Then the righteous will answer Him, 'Lord, when did we see You hungry, and feed You, or thirsty, and give You something to drink?
AMP.38: And when did we see You as a stranger, and invite You in, or naked, and clothe You?
AMP.39: And when did we see You sick, or in prison, and come to You?'
AMP.40: The King will answer and say to them, 'I assure you and most solemnly say to you, to the extent that you did it for one of these brothers of Mine, even the least of them, you did it for Me.'
AMP.41: "Then He will say to those on His left, 'Leave Me, you cursed ones, into the eternal fire which has been prepared for the devil and his angels (demons);
AMP.42: for I was hungry, and you gave Me nothing to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave Me nothing to drink;
AMP.43: I was a stranger, and you did not invite Me in; naked, and you did not clothe Me; sick, and in prison, and you did not visit Me [with help and ministering care].'
AMP.44: Then they also [in their turn] will answer, 'Lord, when did we see You hungry, or thirsty, or as a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister to You?'
AMP.45: Then He will reply to them, 'I assure you and most solemnly say to you, to the extent that you did not do it for one of the least of these [my followers], you did not do it for Me.'
AMP.46: Then these [unbelieving people] will go away into eternal (unending) punishment, but those who are righteous and in right standing with God [will go, by His remarkable grace] into eternal (unending) life."

MSG.1: "God's kingdom is like ten young virgins who took oil lamps and went out to greet the bridegroom.
MSG.2: Five were silly and five were smart.
MSG.3: The silly virgins took lamps, but no extra oil.
MSG.4: The smart virgins took jars of oil to feed their lamps.
MSG.5: The bridegroom didn't show up when they expected him, and they all fell asleep.
MSG.6: "In the middle of the night someone yelled out, 'He's here! The bridegroom's here! Go out and greet him!'
MSG.7: "The ten virgins got up and got their lamps ready.
MSG.8: The silly virgins said to the smart ones, 'Our lamps are going out; lend us some of your oil.'
MSG.9: "They answered, 'There might not be enough to go around; go buy your own.'
MSG.10: "They did, but while they were out buying oil, the bridegroom arrived. When everyone who was there to greet him had gone into the wedding feast, the door was locked.
MSG.11: "Much later, the other virgins, the silly ones, showed up and knocked on the door, saying, 'Master, we're here. Let us in.'
MSG.12: "He answered, 'Do I know you? I don't think I know you.'
MSG.13: "So stay alert. You have no idea when he might arrive.
MSG.14: "It's also like a man going off on an extended trip. He called his servants together and delegated responsibilities.
MSG.15: To one he gave five thousand dollars, to another two thousand, to a third one thousand, depending on their abilities. Then he left.
MSG.16: Right off, the first servant went to work and doubled his master's investment.
MSG.18: But the man with the single thousand dug a hole and carefully buried his master's money.
MSG.19: "After a long absence, the master of those three servants came back and settled up with them.
MSG.20: The one given five thousand dollars showed him how he had doubled his investment.
MSG.21: His master commended him: 'Good work! You did your job well. From now on be my partner.'
MSG.22: "The servant with the two thousand showed how he also had doubled his master's investment.
MSG.23: His master commended him: 'Good work! You did your job well. From now on be my partner.'
MSG.24: "The servant given one thousand said, 'Master, I know you have high standards and hate careless ways, that you demand the best and make no allowances for error.
MSG.25: I was afraid I might disappoint you, so I found a good hiding place and secured your money. Here it is, safe and sound down to the last cent.'
MSG.26: "The master was furious. 'That's a terrible way to live! It's criminal to live cautiously like that! If you knew I was after the best, why did you do less than the least?
MSG.27: The least you could have done would have been to invest the sum with the bankers, where at least I would have gotten a little interest.
MSG.28: " 'Take the thousand and give it to the one who risked the most. And get rid of this "play-it-safe" who won't go out on a limb.
MSG.30: Throw him out into utter darkness.'
MSG.31: "When he finally arrives, blazing in beauty and all his angels with him, the Son of Man will take his place on his glorious throne.
MSG.32: Then all the nations will be arranged before him and he will sort the people out, much as a shepherd sorts out sheep and goats,
MSG.33: putting sheep to his right and goats to his left.
MSG.34: "Then the King will say to those on his right, 'Enter, you who are blessed by my Father! Take what's coming to you in this kingdom. It's been ready for you since the world's foundation.
MSG.35: And here's why: I was hungry and you fed me, I was thirsty and you gave me a drink, I was homeless and you gave me a room,
MSG.36: I was shivering and you gave me clothes, I was sick and you stopped to visit, I was in prison and you came to me.'
MSG.37: "Then those 'sheep' are going to say, 'Master, what are you talking about? When did we ever see you hungry and feed you, thirsty and give you a drink?
MSG.38: And when did we ever see you sick or in prison and come to you?'
MSG.40: Then the King will say, 'I'm telling the solemn truth: Whenever you did one of these things to someone overlooked or ignored, that was me—you did it to me.'
MSG.41: "Then he will turn to the 'goats,' the ones on his left, and say, 'Get out, worthless goats! You're good for nothing but the fires of hell.
MSG.42: And why? Because— I was hungry and you gave me no meal, I was thirsty and you gave me no drink,
MSG.43: I was homeless and you gave me no bed, I was shivering and you gave me no clothes, Sick and in prison, and you never visited.'
MSG.44: "Then those 'goats' are going to say, 'Master, what are you talking about? When did we ever see you hungry or thirsty or homeless or shivering or sick or in prison and didn't help?'
MSG.45: "He will answer them, 'I'm telling the solemn truth: Whenever you failed to do one of these things to someone who was being overlooked or ignored, that was me—you failed to do it to me.'
MSG.46: "Then those 'goats' will be herded to their eternal doom, but the 'sheep' to their eternal reward."

NIV.1:
The Parable of the Ten Virgins
“At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
NIV.2: Five of them were foolish and five were wise.
NIV.3: The foolish ones took their lamps but did not take any oil with them.
NIV.4: The wise, however, took oil in jars along with their lamps.
NIV.5: The bridegroom was a long time in coming, and they all became drowsy and fell asleep.
NIV.6: “At midnight the cry rang out: ‘Here’s the bridegroom! Come out to meet him!’
NIV.7: “Then all the virgins woke up and trimmed their lamps.
NIV.8: The foolish ones said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil; our lamps are going out.’
NIV.9: “‘No,’ they replied, ‘there may not be enough for both us and you. Instead, go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.’
NIV.10: “But while they were on their way to buy the oil, the bridegroom arrived. The virgins who were ready went in with him to the wedding banquet. And the door was shut.
NIV.11: “Later the others also came. ‘Sir! Sir!’ they said. ‘Open the door for us!’
NIV.12: “But he replied, ‘I tell you the truth, I don’t know you.’
NIV.13: “Therefore keep watch, because you do not know the day or the hour.
NIV.14:
The Parable of the Talents
“Again, it will be like a man going on a journey, who called his servants and entrusted his property to them.
NIV.15: To one he gave five talents of money, to another two talents, and to another one talent, each according to his ability. Then he went on his journey.
NIV.16: The man who had received the five talents went at once and put his money to work and gained five more.
NIV.17: So also, the one with the two talents gained two more.
NIV.18: But the man who had received the one talent went off, dug a hole in the ground and hid his master’s money.
NIV.19: “After a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
NIV.20: The man who had received the five talents brought the other five. ‘Master,’ he said, ‘you entrusted me with five talents. See, I have gained five more.’
NIV.21: “His master replied, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Come and share your master’s happiness!’
NIV.22: “The man with the two talents also came. ‘Master,’ he said, ‘you entrusted me with two talents; see, I have gained two more.’
NIV.23: “His master replied, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Come and share your master’s happiness!’
NIV.24: “Then the man who had received the one talent came. ‘Master,’ he said, ‘I knew that you are a hard man, harvesting where you have not sown and gathering where you have not scattered seed.
NIV.25: So I was afraid and went out and hid your talent in the ground. See, here is what belongs to you.’
NIV.26: “His master replied, ‘You wicked, lazy servant! So you knew that I harvest where I have not sown and gather where I have not scattered seed?
NIV.27: Well then, you should have put my money on deposit with the bankers, so that when I returned I would have received it back with interest.
NIV.28: “‘Take the talent from him and give it to the one who has the ten talents.
NIV.29: For everyone who has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him.
NIV.30: And throw that worthless servant outside, into the darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
NIV.31:
The Sheep and the Goats
“When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, he will sit on his throne in heavenly glory.
NIV.32: All the nations will be gathered before him, and he will separate the people one from another as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
NIV.33: He will put the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
NIV.34: “Then the King will say to those on his right, ‘Come, you who are blessed by my Father; take your inheritance, the kingdom prepared for you since the creation of the world.
NIV.35: For I was hungry and you gave me something to eat, I was thirsty and you gave me something to drink, I was a stranger and you invited me in,
NIV.36: I needed clothes and you clothed me, I was sick and you looked after me, I was in prison and you came to visit me.’
NIV.37: “Then the righteous will answer him, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you something to drink?
NIV.38: When did we see you a stranger and invite you in, or needing clothes and clothe you?
NIV.39: When did we see you sick or in prison and go to visit you?’
NIV.40: “The King will reply, ‘I tell you the truth, whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers of mine, you did for me.’
NIV.41: “Then he will say to those on his left, ‘Depart from me, you who are cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels.
NIV.42: For I was hungry and you gave me nothing to eat, I was thirsty and you gave me nothing to drink,
NIV.43: I was a stranger and you did not invite me in, I needed clothes and you did not clothe me, I was sick and in prison and you did not look after me.’
NIV.44: “They also will answer, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or needing clothes or sick or in prison, and did not help you?’
NIV.45: “He will reply, ‘I tell you the truth, whatever you did not do for one of the least of these, you did not do for me.’
NIV.46: “Then they will go away to eternal punishment, but the righteous to eternal life.”

NET.38: When did we see you a stranger and invite you in, or naked and clothe you?
NET.39: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you?’
NET.40: And the king will answer them, ‘I tell you the truth, just as you did it for one of the least of these brothers or sisters of mine, you did it for me.’
NET.41: “Then he will say to those on his left, ‘Depart from me, you accursed, into the eternal fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels!
NET.42: For I was hungry and you gave me nothing to eat, I was thirsty and you gave me nothing to drink.
NET.43: I was a stranger and you did not receive me as a guest, naked and you did not clothe me, sick and in prison and you did not visit me.’
NET.44: Then they too will answer, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and did not give you whatever you needed?’
NET.45: Then he will answer them, ‘I tell you the truth, just as you did not do it for one of the least of these, you did not do it for me.’
NET.46: And these will depart into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”
